beats.narration: Your flaw detector is a measuring instrument, and every echo makes two claims: position measures distance, height measures amplitude. Standardization proves both.

payload.title: TRUST YOUR SCREEN
payload.sub: Proving the electronics before the job starts.

beats.narration: Horizontal linearity first. Back-wall echoes from a parallel block must land at equal spacing — within about two percent of screen width.

payload.heading: Horizontal linearity: the echo ruler
payload.note: EQUAL SPACING · LIMIT ≈ 2% OF SCREEN WIDTH

beats.narration: Vertical linearity next. Lock two echoes at eighty and forty percent, then step the gain. Removing six decibels must halve both — the ratio holds.
Two disciplines protect those checks. Reject stays off — it bends the vertical scale. And verify with the job's exact settings, filter, and cable.

chips.s: Exactly as the job will run

beats.narration: So here's the rule: prove it, record it, then trust it — on the bench, every three months, and after any repair.

payload.sub: Every three months — and after any repair.
